title: Using the P2P Package: A Step-by-Step Tutorial
text: MakeClusterList first step P2P process. function takes dataframe ptmtable runs three calculations statistical measures distance: Euclidean Distance, Spearman Dissimilarity (1- |Spearman Correlation|), SED (average Spearman Dissimilarity (1- Spearman Correlation) Euclidean Distance). Combining two dissimilarities leads better resolution data useful pattern recognition. correlation table— ptm.correlation.matrix—generated based distances calculated pair PTMs. function runs matrices t-SNE generate clusters based previously calculated distance provides cluster list, common.clusters. returned adj.consensus.matrix (identifies PTMs cluster together ‘short distance’ ) ptm.correlation.matrix also used next step create co-cluster correlation networks (CCCNs). three outputs returned list. keeplength paramter defines minimum number PTMs must cluster retained final output. toolong parameter defines maximum distance two PTMs considered clustering together. MakeClusterList can run like :    following unpacks output separate objects discussed : Now can view objects. First, example cluster: Next, look piece adjacency matrix. Ones represent pair cluster zeroes represent pair doesn’t: part PTM correlation matrix. Values pairs PTMs Spearman correlation coefficients ranging -1 1. two PTMs experimental conditions common, correlation value NA.

what: Step 4: Build PPI Network and Cluster Filtered Network
title: Using the P2P Package: A Step-by-Step Tutorial
text: BuildClusterFilteredNetwork function allows users filter protein-protein interaction networks using previously generated co-cluster correlation networks. PPIs retained cluster filtered network (CFN) interacting proteins share statistically correlated PTMs identified via t-SNE clusters. BuildClusterFilteredNetwork function combines PPI data downloaded step 3 efficiently possible retaining desired edge weights. normalizes weights scale 0-1 gives output cluster filter network retain interacting proteins whose genes within co-cluster correlation network created step 2. first run function: unpack outputs separate variables: can view portion CFN : reduce clutter graphs, CFN edges can merged. collapses two edges two nodes single edge, combining edge names:

"))      if (any(fixgenes %in% cellv)) {          cellv.new <- gsub(fixgenes[fixgenes %in% cellv],                       corrects[fixgenes %in% cellv], cellv)     return (paste(cellv.new, collapse="; "))        } else return(cell)   } name.peptide <- function (genes, modification="p", sites, aa, pepsep=";")   {   genes.v <- unlist(strsplit(genes, pepsep, fixed = TRUE))   genes.v[which(genes.v == "NA")] <- NA   genes.v <- genes.v[!is.na(genes.v)]      sites.v <- unlist(strsplit(sites, pepsep, fixed = TRUE))   sites.v[which(sites.v == "NA")] <- NA   sites.v <- sites.v[!is.na(sites.v)]   sites.v <- sapply(sites.v, function (x) paste (aa, x, sep=""))      Peptide.v <- as.character(noquote(paste(     genes.v[1:length(genes.v)], modification,     sites.v[1:length(sites.v)], sep=" ")))      Peptide <- paste(unique(Peptide.v), collapse="; ")      return(Peptide) } # file_path <- "path/to/your/file.txt" file_path <- system.file("extdata", "phospho_cleaned_mapped.txt",                          package = "PTMsToPathways")  newphos <- utils::read.table(file_path, sep = "\t", skip = 0, header = TRUE,                              blank.lines.skip = T, fill = T, quote = "\"", dec = ".",                              comment.char = "", stringsAsFactors = F) dim(newphos) >> [1] 933 170 newphos <- newphos[!is.na(newphos$AllGeneSymbols),] dim(newphos) >> [1] 908 170 newphos$AllGeneSymbols <- sapply(newphos$AllGeneSymbols, fix.excel) headercols <- c("AllGeneSymbols", "Amino.Acid",                 "Positions.Within.Proteins", "Modification.Type") newphos.head <- newphos[,headercols] newphos.head$Peptide.Name <- mapply(   name.peptide, genes = newphos.head$AllGeneSymbols,   sites =  newphos.head$Positions.Within.Proteins, aa = newphos.head$Amino.Acid)

title: Graphing and Manipulating Cluster Filtered Network — getFuncKey
text: Creates cytoscape graph cluster filtered network. Ensure Cytoscape app open RCy3 package downloaded. package RCy3 required many functions script. download, run: (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("RCy3") also recommended (required) users load RCy3 library(RCy3) access functions directly.

title: Graphing and Manipulating Cluster Filtered Network — getFuncKey
code: getFuncKey(funckey.filename = "FunctionKey.txt")

title: Graphing and Manipulating Cluster Filtered Network — getFuncKey
text: cfn version ppi.network edges exist cccn.matrix non-zero weights ptmtable dataset post-translational modifications. Formatted numbered rows, first column containing PTM names. rest column names drugs. Values numeric values represent much PTM reacted drug. funckey table graphing gene names type protein; defaults internal database PTMsToPathways::ex.funckey Network.title Desired title created Cytoscape Network; defaults "cfn" Network.collection Desired name collection created Cytoscape network reside; defaults "PTMsToPathways" visual.style.name Desired name visual style created Cytoscape; defaults "PTMsToPathways.style" background.color Hex code background color graph; defaults '#fcf3cf' edge.label.color Hex code edge label color graph; defaults '#17202a' node.label.color Hex code node label color graph; defaults '#145a32' default.font Font style edge node names; defaults "Times New Roman" node.font.size Font size node name; defaults 12 edge.font.size Font size edge name; defaults 8 edge.line.style Type edge style; defaults "SOLID"; options include: "PARALLEL_LINES", "MARQUEE_EQUAL", "DOT", "EQUAL_DASH", "LONG_DASH", "CONTIGUOUS_ARROW", "MARQUEE_DASH", "DASH_DOT", "BACKWARD_SLASH", "FORWARD_SLASH", "VERTICAL_SLASH", "SOLID", "SEPARATE_ARROW", "MARQUEE_DASH_DOT", "ZIGZAG", "SINEWAVE" edge.opacity Opacity edge line scale 0 - 255 0 transparent; defaults 175 edge.label.opacity Opacity edge label scale 0 - 255 0 transparent; defaults 255 border.opacity Opacity node border scale 0 - 255 0 transparent; defaults 255 node.label.opacity Opacity node label scale 0 - 255 0 transparent; defaults 255 node.fill.opacity Opacity node fill scale 0 - 255 0 transparent; defaults 255

title: Extracts co-clustered PTM and gene network from a cluster filtered network object. — ptms_to_cfn
code: # Example input objects ptms <- c("TP53 p S15", "BRCA1 p S123; BRCA1 p T124", "MDM2 p S200") # Example network # edge list with 'source', 'target', and cluster info, could be data.frame or igraph cfn.merged <- data.frame(source = c("TP53", "BRCA1", "BRCA1", "MDM2"),                          target = c("BRCA1", "MDM2", "TP53", "TP53"),                          cluster = c(1,1,2,1)) # Suppose filter.edges.0 and get.co.clustered.ptms are also defined and loaded # The following returns the gene/PTM subnetwork res <- ptms_to_cfn(ptms, cfn = cfn.merged, pepsep = ";") #> Error in ptms_to_cfn(ptms, cfn = cfn.merged, pepsep = ";"): object 'cfn.merged' not found print(res) #> Error: object 'res' not found
